title: Mohammed M'Hiri
content:
People.ContactEmail mmhiri@mccarthy.ca
- University of Toronto
Mohammed M’Hiri is an associate in our Technology Group in Toronto. He provides practical solutions to clients across a broad range of commercial transactions, including those involving technology outsourcing, software‑as‑a‑service (SaaS), licensing, privacy and data‑sharing, and consumer contracts. He also advises clients on the technology, privacy, and transitional services aspects of merger and acquisition transactions. Mohammed’s aim in every engagement is to work closely with clients to deliver pragmatic legal solutions that address the risks inherent in the particular transaction and technology.
Prior to joining the firm, Mohammed spent a summer at a boutique firm specializing in Intellectual Property in Ottawa and then summered and completed his Articles at a leading international law firm in Toronto.
Mohammed holds a BASc in Electrical Engineering (Hons) from the University of Toronto. He received his JD from the University of Toronto in 2023, where he was the recipient of numerous individual course prizes. He also served as an executive of the Technology and Intellectual Property Club and as co‑president of the Muslim Law Students Association. Mohammed is a longstanding believer in the importance of community building and youth engagement.
Mohammed was called to the Ontario bar in 2024 and is a Certified Information Privacy Professional (Canada).
